build.determinism_note: Scope N2-A.1: workflow run 29371996936 found an intermittent producer-ordering defect (Roslyn shared-compiler-server scheduling caused CoreCompile diagnostics to interleave in different orders across independent builds, though the full warning multiset, exit code, and every other semantic field always matched). --disable-build-servers alone scored deterministic (6/6) in determinism_probe.py's same-runner, unconfined probe, but FAILED when actually proven across a real, separately-provisioned, Sandboy-confined capture-a/capture-b pair (workflow run 29374881325) — the probe's same-runner, unconfined methodology did not fully predict behavior under Sandboy confinement on genuinely independent runners. The full combination of all five tested flags (-p:UseSharedCompilation=false, --disable-build-servers, -m:1, -p:BuildInParallel=false, -p:RunAnalyzersInParallel=false) is adopted instead, and is being re-verified against the real Sandboy-confined two-runner pipeline before acceptance. See n2a-determinism-probe-report.json and n2a-hotfix-summary.md for the complete evidentiary trail, including the falsified minimal-flag attempt.

notes: Selected manually by explicit user approval outside this session's automated discovery (N2-A has no RepoSelector/CandidateScorer).
